chunks.text: Olivia Hsu to give speech at national IEEE-HKN virtual graduation celebration - EECS at Berkeley EECS alumna Olivia Hsu (B.S. ’19) will be giving a speech at the national 2020 IEEE-Eta Kappa Nu (HKN) virtual graduation celebration on Saturday, May 30, 2020. Hsu is the winner of the 2019 IEEE-HKN Alton B. Zerby and Carl T. Koerner Outstanding Electrical or Computer Engineering Student of the Year Award, and is the representative for the Mu (Berkeley) Chapter, which has won the IEEE-HKN Outstanding Chapter Award every year since 2001. While at Berkeley, Hsu co-founded the student group Space Technologies at Cal (STAC) and won the 2019 EECS Arthur M. Hopkin award, which recognizes outstanding EE undergraduates who “demonstrate seriousness of purpose and high academic achievement.” She is currently pursuing a Ph.D. at Stanford with a focus on computer architecture, digital circuits, and computer systems. IEEE-HKN will host the event for the first time this year in place of the campus commencement ceremonies which have been cancelled nationwide.
